sourceMovie.title: I See You
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto I See You (2019) as a horror theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Gone Girl
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If I See You clicked for you, Gone Girl (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and mystery, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Wake Up Dead Man: A Knives Out Mystery
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If I See You clicked for you, Wake Up Dead Man: A Knives Out Mystery (2025) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and mystery, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Eden Lake
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If I See You clicked for you, Eden Lake (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after I See You?
faq.answer: Try Gone Girl, Wake Up Dead Man: A Knives Out Mystery, Eden Lake—each shares DNA with I See You while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is I See You?
faq.answer: I See You (2019) is best described as Horror, Thriller, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is I See You worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
